skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit images with RunComfy using model-specific routes for text-to-image, image-to-image, typography, portraits, brand assets, and fast concept work.
skill.summary: Routes image generation and editing requests to suitable RunComfy models with ready CLI commands.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, network, filesystem, weak crypto, and path traversal indicators. Most hits are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline model identifiers, placeholder URLs, and documentation text; no prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found. Confirmed risk is medium because the skill authorizes Bash(runcomfy *), sends prompts and references to RunComfy, stores an API token locally, and downloads generated output files.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution and Remote Image API Use
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash(runcomfy *) and provides runcomfy run examples. The documentation states the CLI sends requests to the RunComfy Model API, polls status, fetches results, and downloads generated files. This is intended behavior, but user prompts, reference URLs, and generated outputs leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration and How it works section directly confirm external command execution and network API behavior. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, so the risk is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Generated File Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents token storage in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json and writes generated outputs to a chosen output directory. The token file mode is documented as 0600 and generated downloads have a size cap, but credential storage and file writes still require user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact token path, output directory behavior, and download cap are explicitly described. The documentation includes reasonable mitigations, so this is a controlled operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Reference Content Can Influence Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill warns that reference image URLs and web search results are untrusted and can steer generation through embedded text, metadata, or external grounding. It recommends explicit user-provided URLs and defaults web search to false, which mitigates but does not remove the semantic risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly describes the untrusted input boundary and mitigation steps. This is a realistic content-influence risk rather than evidence of malicious skill behavior.

low_findings.title: Package Manager Setup Commands Require Operator Trust
low_findings.description: The documentation shows npm and npx setup commands for the RunComfy CLI. The security section limits routine skill execution to runcomfy and warns against piping arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell, so this is a supply-chain consideration rather than confirmed abuse.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install commands are present and package manager trust is a real concern. The risk is reduced because allowed-tools is scoped to runcomfy and the file explicitly warns against unreviewed remote install scripts.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Pattern Noise in Markdown Documentation
low_findings.description: Most reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and path traversal indicators are false positives from Markdown inline code, code fences, model identifiers, quoted strings, and placeholder URLs. No Ruby code, dynamic shell construction, cryptographic implementation, or local path traversal behavior was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the cited areas are command examples, inline endpoint names, and placeholder HTTPS URLs. The semantic context does not support the scanner labels.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Namespace for runcomfy
dangerous_patterns.description: The front matter grants Bash access for runcomfy commands. This is necessary for the skill, but it is still an external command capability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is explicit and narrow. It confirms command capability without showing arbitrary shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Calls and Downloads
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill states that the CLI posts to the RunComfy Model API, polls status, fetches results, and downloads generated URLs from RunComfy domains.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is directly documented and matches the intended image generation workflow. No unrelated callback or telemetry endpoint was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Token File Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login writes an API token under the user config directory with mode 0600.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token location and permissions are explicitly stated. This is credential handling, but not evidence of exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Install Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup section includes npm and npx commands for installing or invoking the CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clear package-manager examples. They create supply-chain exposure, but the skill scope and security notes reduce concern.

content.value_statement: Choosing the right image model can slow creative production. This skill routes generation and editing requests to RunComfy models with practical prompts and CLI commands.
actual_capabilities: Selects RunComfy text-to-image routes for typography, portraits, product images, concept art, and fast drafts.
Provides runcomfy CLI command patterns for supported image models and endpoints.
Covers image-to-image edit routes for identity-preserving edits, multilingual text replacement, and precise single-image changes.
Documents model-specific parameters such as size, aspect ratio, resolution, seed, steps, and output format.
Explains prompt patterns for exact text, subject-first descriptions, reference images, and batch ideation.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy login or token.
Sends prompts, reference URLs, and generated results through RunComfy services.
Does not cover full mask-driven inpainting; it points users to a separate image-edit skill.
Model schemas and availability depend on the current RunComfy catalog.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate posters, product scenes, and brand concepts with model choices for text accuracy or photoreal style.

use_cases.title: Produce Fast Concept Boards
use_cases.description: Use fast model routes and batch settings to explore many visual directions before selecting a final style.

use_cases.title: Edit Existing Images
use_cases.description: Route reference-based edits to models that preserve identity, update backgrounds, or replace visible text.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a square image of a ceramic coffee mug on a marble counter in warm morning light. Use a fast draft model first.

prompt_templates.title: Create Text-Accurate Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Poster or ad with exact visible words
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landscape product poster with the headline reading exactly "AURORA Spring 2026". Use the best route for accurate in-image text.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the provided portrait so the subject identity, pose, and clothing stay unchanged. Replace only the background with a rainy neon street.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Model Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a RunComfy workflow for ten campaign concepts, one final photoreal hero image, and localized poster versions in three languages.

output_examples.input: Generate a poster with exact headline text for a product launch.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the typography-focused route, explains the size choice, and provides a ready RunComfy command pattern.

output_examples.input: Create several fast moodboard options for a ceramic mug campaign.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a fast draft model, batch settings, aspect ratio guidance, and prompt wording for quick comparison.

output_examples.input: Change a portrait background while keeping the subject unchanged.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the task to an image-to-image edit model and frames the prompt around preservation before describing the change.

best_practices: Start with the subject, then describe scene, lighting, composition, and final style.
Use exact quoted text when visible words must appear in the generated image.
Keep web search and external reference URLs limited to assets the user explicitly approved.
anti_patterns: Do not pass arbitrary dimensions to models that only support fixed size values.
Do not use high-resolution settings for early ideation when draft settings are enough.
Do not include secrets, private images, or unapproved reference URLs in prompts.

faq.question: Does this skill create images directly?
faq.answer: It prepares and runs RunComfy CLI commands that call RunComfy image models.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it support editing existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes image-to-image routes for reference-based edits and visible text replacement.

faq.question: Can it handle exact text in images?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends GPT Image 2 routes for posters, ads, and multilingual visible text.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: Users need the RunComfy CLI and either an interactive login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN value.

faq.question: Are prompts sent to a third party?
faq.answer: Yes. The RunComfy CLI sends prompts and references to the RunComfy Model API over HTTPS.
